short_definition: An intelligence-backed, strictly bounded, read-only assessment of whether a governed task is executable in a declared environment.
description: TaskExecutabilityAssessment captures a low-cognition, read-only evaluator's structured judgment about a task snapshot and the declared environment in which it would be executed. The result is a verdict (executable, needs_revision, or not_executable) with findings that must be mechanically derivable from evidence. It is not a guarantee of correctness, success, or value; it only answers whether the task as written can be attempted with the declared authority and tools.
boundaries: Task Lifecycle owns assessment truth and policy, not Delegation or NARS.
Delegation owns outcome orchestration; Worker Delegation owns provider/runtime execution.
NARS and Site Loop dispatch and reconcile, but never own the request authority.
Provider/model selection is resolved by Delegation registry, not Task Lifecycle policy.
A missing or failed evaluator is not a verdict about the task; execution state is separate from assessment verdict.

authority.notes: Contracts, deterministic digests, policy resolution, and mechanical verdict derivation live in the neutral task-governance-core package. Storage, MCP tools, NARS hooks, Site Loop steps, and Delegated Task templates are owned by their respective surfaces.

examples: on_create policy triggers an assessment request when a task is created
strict enforcement refuses a task-linked delegation when no current executable assessment exists
one-shot operator override authorizes a single dispatch with a changed environment
counterexamples: asking a worker whether the task is a good idea
treating a failed provider as a needs_revision verdict
embedding provider/model names in Task Lifecycle policy

confidence.basis: Contracts, policy resolution, digests, and tests are implemented; downstream storage, orchestration, and enforcement are pending as tasks 2224-2230.
